meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: User Onboarding to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Learn how to leverage user onboarding to overcome stagnant project ideation. An expert guide for founders using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop searching for the 'perfect' idea. Instead, build a robust onboarding flow for a niche problem you've identified. By focusing on user friction, you uncover real-world pain points that demand solutions. Use Antigravity to scaffold your architecture and Claude Code to iterate rapidly on high-conversion signup experiences.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of ambition. You’re trapped in the 'no project idea' loop, paralyzed by the fear of building something nobody wants. Most founders treat ideation as a mystical search, when in reality, it is an engineering problem—specifically, a friction problem.

The secret isn't a better idea; it's a better feedback loop. By building a high-intent user onboarding flow, you stop guessing and start observing. You aren't just building a feature; you are building a diagnostic tool that forces users to reveal exactly what they are willing to pay for.

steps.title: Architecting the Hook
steps.description: Define the 'Aha!' moment. We need to map out the exact sequence of steps a user takes to reach the core value proposition of your MVP, focusing on minimal data collection.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a lean, high-converting onboarding schema for a SaaS MVP. Include fields for user pain-point identification, industry vertical, and primary goal. Ensure the data model supports quick iteration.

steps.title: Implementing the Progressive Profiling
steps.description: Don't overwhelm the user. Implement a multi-step form that captures data incrementally to keep drop-off rates low.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a React component for a multi-step onboarding wizard. Include state management for form progression and validation logic for each step.

steps.title: Integrating Real-Time Feedback
steps.description: Pipe onboarding responses into a dashboard so you can see exactly where users get stuck and what problems they are trying to solve.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a backend service that captures onboarding form submissions and pushes them to a summary dashboard, identifying key user intent trends.

steps.title: Automating the Value Delivery
steps.description: Trigger an automated email or notification based on the user's specific pain point captured during onboarding to prove value immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a webhook handler that triggers a personalized email sequence based on the 'user_pain_point' field in the onboarding submission.

steps.title: Optimizing for Conversion
steps.description: Use Claude Code to run A/B tests on your onboarding copy to see which framing leads to higher activation rates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate an A/B testing wrapper for the onboarding component that tracks conversion rates per variation.

pro_tips: Never ask for a password until the user has experienced the 'Aha!' moment.
Use onboarding as a survey tool to validate your next feature set before you write a line of code.
Track the time-to-value (TTV) specifically during the onboarding flow; if it exceeds 60 seconds, you are losing money.
Implement 'Empty State' onboarding where the interface teaches the user how to solve their problem as they build.
Always include a 'Skip' option for power users to prevent churn during the first 30 seconds.
content.next_steps: Deploy your onboarding flow to a staging environment, run 10 test users through it, and analyze the drop-off points to refine your core value proposition.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to turn your onboarding process into a high-octane research engine. Stop stalling and start building the bridge between user pain and your revenue. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
